question: Explain the difference between `public`, `private`, and `protected` access specifiers in C++ classes. Provide a brief code snippet demonstrating each in a simple class structure.

test_cases.expected:
================================================================
  EXPLANATION OF C++ ACCESS SPECIFIERS (READABILITY/EFFICIENCY ISSUE)
================================================================
Access specifiers (public, private, protected) are keywords in C++ that define the accessibility of a class's members.
  * Public: Members declared as public are accessible from anywhere. They constitute the class's interface.
  * Private: Members declared as private are only accessible from within the class itself. They are hidden from outside world and derived classes.
  * Protected: Members declared as protected are accessible from within the class itself and from its derived classes. They are not directly accessible from outside.
================================================================

--- Demonstrating ExcessiveVerbosityAccessControlDemonstratorClass ---
  [Public Method Call]: Displaying Public Information: This is a string that is public and can be accessed from any part of the program.
    [Private Method Call]: Processing Private Data: This string holds very sensitive private information that should never be exposed externally.
      [Protected Method Call]: Utilizing Protected Functionality: This string contains protected state information meant for derived classes.
  [Public Method Call]: Just another public function doing nothing much.
  Directly accessing public data from main: This is a string that is public and can be accessed from any part of the program.

--- Demonstrating DerivedExcessiveVerbosityDemonstrator ---
  [Derived Class Constructor]: DerivedExcessiveVerbosityDemonstrator object created.
  [Public Method Call]: Displaying Public Information: This is a string that is public and can be accessed from any part of the program.
    [Private Method Call]: Processing Private Data: This string holds very sensitive private information that should never be exposed externally.
      [Protected Method Call]: Utilizing Protected Functionality: This string contains protected state information meant for derived classes.
    [Derived Method Call]: Attempting to access protected members from base class...
      Derived can access protected string: This string contains protected state information meant for derived classes.
      Derived can access protected double: 3.14159
      [Protected Method Call]: Utilizing Protected Functionality: This string contains protected state information meant for derived classes.
      [Protected Method Call]: Yet another protected service method executed.
  Directly accessing inherited public data from derived object: This is a string that is public and can be accessed from any part of the program.

================================================================
  END OF DEMONSTRATION
================================================================
